newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Shares went ex-dividend NT$20.00 on June 8 (payable June 30), a ~0.5% administrative event. The strong Q1 2026 print (revenue NT$11.4b, +63% YoY; EPS NT$12.28), raised FY26 consensus (~NT$52.3b revenue), and the May 26 VSORA Jotunn8 turnkey ASIC showcase at TSMC Europe Symposium all predate the window. Today's +1.29% move is flow/drift, not fundamental.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - Global Unichip (Q1 2026 results, NT$20 dividend ex-date Jun 8, FY26 consensus revision)
GlobeNewswire - GUC showcases VSORA Jotunn8 AI inference processor (May 26, 2026)
TrendForce - CSPs accelerate ASIC push in 2H26; MediaTek, GUC, Alchip benefit

verdicts.rankRationale: On the node-centrality lens GUC's position as TSMC-affiliated custom-ASIC turnkey node is unchanged; no new design wins, capacity news, or guidance since June 6, and a +1.29% drift day does not alter its 'selective, less direct than HBM/packaging' placement at rank 15.
verdicts.bull: CSP custom-silicon buildouts accelerate into 2H26 with GUC (TSMC 34.8% holder) capturing 3nm/2nm turnkey and chiplet/HBM interface work; FY26 consensus revenue already lifted to ~NT$52.3b and could prove conservative.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully visible: Q1 beat and raised estimates are in the price after a 97% YTD run; node criticality is real yet secondary to HBM, CoWoS, and networking pure-plays on this page.
verdicts.bear: Customer/project concentration plus NRE-to-turnkey timing lumpiness; a single delayed or cancelled hyperscaler ASIC program would dent FY27 turnkey revenue while the stock trades on flawless execution.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May/June monthly revenue releases and new 2nm/chiplet ASIC design-win disclosures into 2H26.
verdicts.primaryRisk: ASIC project cancellation or push-out at a concentrated customer compressing turnkey revenue.

verdicts.rankRationale: Crowding-sensitive alpha lens: the AI-ASIC design-services theme is now consensus (TrendForce et al. flag GUC/Alchip/MediaTek as the obvious beneficiaries) and the stock has rerated 97% YTD to an $18B cap with zero new fundamental evidence since June 6 - residual alpha is shrinking, so it should drift down from rank 22.
verdicts.bull: Hyperscaler custom-silicon pipelines are multi-year and GUC's TSMC alignment gives it privileged access to N3/N2 and advanced packaging; any fresh marquee design win could re-open upside despite the run.
verdicts.neutral: Yesterday's hold was right on the news, but on a post-rerating alpha list a doubled stock with no incremental evidence is a worse setup than a week ago even if the business is fine.
verdicts.bear: At ~NT$4,310 the stock discounts the raised FY26 numbers; the name is widely owned as 'the' Taiwan ASIC-services proxy, so any NRE lull, share loss to Alchip/Broadcom-direct, or hyperscaler insourcing triggers a sharp de-rating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A new disclosed hyperscaler or AI-startup turnkey win that resets the FY27 revenue base.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded positioning into decelerating news flow - valuation gives back multiple faster than estimates rise.
